message:
br-4qom2: enforce tmux cmd_queue cap at push sites, not only at pop

ft-4qom2: TmuxDomainState.cmd_queue had CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000
enforced only inside send_next_command (tmux.rs:289) via
cmd_queue.drain(..excess). send_next_command early-returns at line 286
when state != Idle, so the drain never fires if tmux is stuck in
WaitingForResponse.

Concrete interleaving reaching the unbounded-growth state:
  1. tmux sends a command, enters State::WaitingForResponse.
  2. tmux server stalls (remote tmux on lagging SSH, overloaded server,
     dropped Guarded response).
  3. GUI user resizes window repeatedly; each Event::LayoutChange pushes
     a ListAllPanes command onto cmd_queue without a cap check.
  4. send_next_command never runs past its state-gate; no drain.
  5. cmd_queue grows unbounded -> eventual OOM.

Four push sites were vulnerable:
  - Event::LayoutChange         -> ListAllPanes    (line ~196)
  - Event::SessionChanged       -> ListCommands    (line ~223)
  - Event::WindowAdd            -> ListAllWindows  (line ~234)
  - split_pane (user split)     -> SplitPane       (line ~375)

Fix: extract the drain-oldest-plus-log logic from send_next_command into
a push_command_capped(queue, cmd) helper that runs atomically with each
push_back under the existing cmd_queue lock. Route all four sites
through it. No additional locking cost (caller already holds the lock).

The existing hard_cap_drops_oldest_when_exceeded test at line 870 is
untouched: it uses raw queue.push_back to intentionally bypass the
push-site cap, then asserts the pop-site drain still works as a
safety-net. Both layers now exist — the push-site cap is the primary
defense, the pop-site drain remains the belt-and-suspenders fallback.

cargo check --manifest-path frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml --offline on
/tmp/ft-pane1-target passes clean in 1m41s.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
